SPY.narrative: Opened at $638.94 (gap down), rallied $17+ to $656.25 high, now $654.57. Second consecutive V-shaped reversal from gap-down open.

QQQ.narrative: Tech rallying on risk-on sentiment and OpenAI funding round. Nasdaq +0.96%.

IWM.narrative: Russell 2000 continuing broad risk-on rotation from yesterday's +3.41% surge.

VIX.note: Continuing compression from 30.61 (March 31 open) to 24-25 range. Regime transition from HIGH to NORMAL confirmed.

WTI_crude.note: Volatile - war premium unwinding partially on peace hopes, but Hormuz still closed

sentiment.note: 3rd consecutive day at extreme fear. MoneyMorning reports possible single-digit reading. Historical pattern: single-digit readings in April 2025 and November 2025 both marked significant bottoms.

catalysts.primary: Iran de-escalation - Trump said US forces leaving in 2-3 weeks
secondary: OpenAI funding round backed by Nvidia, Amazon, SoftBank - bullish AI
ADP jobs +62k vs +40k est (goldilocks: resilient but cooling)
Oil war premium unwinding (WTI pulling back from $107 swing high)
Q1 ends with major rally - Dow +1125 pts on March 31
risks: Iran headline reversal could spike VIX back above 28
Strait of Hormuz still closed - oil supply risk remains
SPY still -5.16% YTD despite recent rally
